content.problemStatement:
For an array BBB of length MMM, we define its value f(B)f(B)f(B) as follows:

f(B)=‚àëi=1M‚àí1((‚àëj=1iBj)‚àí(‚àëj=i+1MBj))f(B) = \sum_{i=1}^{M-1} \left( \left( \sum_{j=1}^i B_j\right) - \left( \sum_{j=i+1}^M B_j \right) \right)f(B)=i=1‚àëM‚àí1‚Äã((j=1‚àëi‚ÄãBj‚Äã)‚àí(j=i+1‚àëM‚ÄãBj‚Äã))In particular, if M=1M = 1M=1 then f(B)=0f(B) = 0f(B)=0.

You're given an array AAA of length NNN.

Find the sum of values of all of its subarrays.

That is, compute

‚àëL=1N‚àëR=LNf(A[L‚Ä¶R])\sum_{L=1}^N \sum_{R=L}^N f(A[L\ldots R])L=1‚àëN‚ÄãR=L‚àëN‚Äãf(A[L‚Ä¶R])where A[L‚Ä¶R]A[L\ldots R]A[L‚Ä¶R] denotes the subarray [AL,AL+1,AL+2,‚Ä¶,AR][A_L, A_{L+1}, A_{L+2}, \ldots, A_R][AL‚Äã,AL+1‚Äã,AL+2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äã].

The answer can be large, so print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of values of all subarrays of the array, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^5.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‚Äëseparated integers A1, A2, ‚Ä¶, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the sum of f(B) over all subarrays B of A, taken modulo 998244353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first testcase, we can have only size 1 subarray and hence answer comes out to be 0.
In the second testcase, we can find the final sum and that comes out to be 6.
In the third testcase, our sum comes negative and hence the value after applying modulo operator becomes 998244347

solution.approach:
For a subarray B of length M we have
    f(B) = \sum_{i=1}^{M-1} (2¬∑prefix_i - total)
where prefix_i is the sum of the first i elements of B and total is the sum of all elements of B.

Using prefix sums we can rewrite the contribution of each original array element A[t] to the final answer. After swapping summations we obtain a closed form for the total contribution of position t (1‚Äëbased):
    contribution(t) = A[t] ¬∑ t ¬∑ X ¬∑ ( (X‚Äë1) - (N‚Äë1)/2 )
where X = N‚Äët+1 and division by 2 is performed modulo MOD (using the modular inverse of 2).

Thus the answer for one test case can be computed in O(N) by iterating over t, evaluating the formula with modular arithmetic and summing the results. The overall complexity is O( Œ£N ) with O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O( Œ£N ) over the whole input.
